title: ammannia auriculata f. undulata
intro: Ammannia auriculata f. undulata is a plant species that belongs to the Acanthaceae family and is commonly known as the 'Golden Dew' or 'Creeping Jenny'. Traditionally, it is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and skin regeneration. Ammannia auriculata f. undulata is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
